content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Found one robustness issue: covered-by comment deduplication uses substring matching on raw `amendment_id`, which can produce false-positive suppression of required comments.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1216` uses `comment.text.contains(&amendment.amendment_id)` when deduplicating covered-by reconciliation comments. This is substring-based and can suppress a legitimate new comment if an unrelated existing comment already contains that token, so an important target-bead linkage can be skipped on first post for the run.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Idempotency check is too permissive and can create false positives; checking the explicit `amendment_id=<id>` marker (or a structured marker field) avoids missed comments.
